source_jobs.title: Director of E-Commerce
source_jobs.description:
Director of E-Commerce

Type: Full-Time

Location: Remote

Work Timezone: U.S. / North America

Compensation: $3,000-$5,500/month USD



Our Client:

Our client is building the next generation functional cookie company reimagining cookies as snacks with real benefits. Their brand is built on the belief that flavor comes first and function should never feel like a compromise. They are launching with three functional SKUs across Focus, Fuel, and Sleep, and building a brand designed to scale into a modern food conglomerate spanning DTC and retail. We are weeks from launch and entering our most important execution phase. Our site is not just a store. It is the engine of our brand, community, and revenue.



The Role:

They are hiring a Director of E-Commerce to build and own the entire digital revenue engine of the brand. You will take us from zero to launch, from launch to $50k+ monthly revenue, and from early traction to a scalable DTC and omnichannel machine. You will own the website, the funnel, the growth strategy, and the performance outcomes. This is a builder role. You will architect the system and personally operate it.



What You Will Own:

Launch, revenue, and financial ownership

• Lead all DTC workstreams to support our 4/7 launch

• Own hitting at least $50k in DTC revenue in month one

• Build systems to drive 10 to 20% month over month growth

• Own DTC revenue forecasting, growth planning, and performance pacing

• Own DTC budget management, channel spend, and return on investment

• Partner with Ops and leadership to manage contribution margins and channel level

profitability



Site ownership and merchandising

• Own Shopify end-to-end

• Build conversion focused PDPs, bundles, and merchandising

• Partner closely with brand and creative to bring launches to life

• Continuously optimize site experience to improve conversion, AOV, and repeat purchase



Subscription, gifting, and retention

• Build and optimize subscription from day one

• Design gifting experiences, bundles, and seasonal programs

• Own retention, repeat purchase, LTV, and cohort performance

• Build loyalty, referral, and community driven commerce programs



Performance, affiliate, and growth marketing

• Lead performance marketing strategy and execution

• Own the full digital growth stack including paid social, search, affiliate, creators,

marketplaces, email, SMS, and emerging channels

• Build and scale our affiliate and creator commerce engine

• Launch and manage affiliate platforms, partnerships, and commission structures

• Recruit, enable, and optimize creators and partners to drive performance based revenue

• Manage agencies or freelancers as needed

• Own CAC, ROAS, LTV, and payback

• Rapidly test channels, creatives, offers, and landing experiences



Lifecycle, SEO, and search AI

• Own email and SMS strategy

• Build high performing flows and campaigns

• Own SEO and organic growth from zero

• Develop the SEO roadmap across site structure, content, PDPs, and technical foundations

• Develop a search forward and AI search strategy to ensure the brand is discoverable

across emerging AI driven search platforms

• Partner with content and brand teams to create high intent, high converting organic traffic

engines



New channels and marketplaces

• Own TikTok Shop and Amazon from a digital commerce and growth standpoint

• Build merchandising, traffic, and conversion strategies specific to each marketplace

• Lead storefront experience, creative testing, and promotional strategy

• Partner with Ops to scale inventory, fulfillment, and channel expansion

• Own revenue, CAC, and contribution margin performance on each marketplace

Customer experience and service

• Own the customer service experience across DTC and marketplaces

• Select and manage customer service tools, partners, or platforms

• Build support workflows, macros, and service standards

• Use customer insights to inform site optimization, retention programs, and

source_jobs.title: Operations Coordinator
source_jobs.description:
Type: Full-Time

Location: Remote

Work Timezone: U.S. / North America

Compensation: $1,100-$1,500/month USD

 

About Marco: Marco connects the top 1% of global professionals with ambitious, fast-growing companies. We believe the future of work is borderless—and that exceptional people come from anywhere.



The Role & Our Client:

Our client is a fast-growing Mediterranean restaurant franchise, with 7 operating locations and 10 additional locations launching over the next 12–18 months. They're hiring an Operations Coordinator to take ownership of the backend systems and recurring operational workflows that keep their multi-location network running smoothly.

If you're a detail-oriented operator who loves bringing order to multi-tool workflows, communicating across a multi-location network, and finding ways to make processes better, faster, and simpler — this is a high-trust role with significant room to grow as the brand expands.



Responsibilities - What You'll Do:

 - Operations tools management: Own the day-to-day backend across the brand's operational stack: Toast POS, Super Order, Opus LMS, Appfront Loyalty, Customer.io http://Customer.io and ClickUp. Keep forms, checklists, and project workflows organized and current across all locations.

 - Location ops oversight: Review daily opening checklists submitted by each location — including fryer oil quality, station readiness, and equipment status — and flag issues to franchisees in real time.

 - Inventory & data integrity: Manage inventory submissions across all locations. Spot anomalies, follow up on missing submissions, and ensure data integrity across the network.

 - SOP creation & maintenance: Build and maintain SOPs that codify how each location should operate, working closely with the Chief of Staff and Director of Operations.

 - Forms & reporting: Syndicate weekly employee feedback forms, collect submissions, chase missing data, and reformat outputs into clean weekly reports for leadership.

 - Delivery platform management: Handle backend menu, listing, and operational accuracy on Uber Eats, DoorDash, and other delivery channels.

 - Community management: Light social monitoring and response on Facebook, Instagram, and TikTok comments, leveraging AI-assisted workflows.

 - Continuous improvement: Surface process improvement ideas. The brand is in growth mode — this role is for someone who wants to use their brain, not just execute tickets.

 - CEO Support:
   
   - Calendar support
   
   - Inbox triage
   
   - Meeting notes
   
   - Follow-up tracking
   
   - ClickUp action items
   
   - CEO priority tracker
   
   - Light research and special projects

Must Have:

 - Fluent English: C1/C2 level (written and spoken).

 - 1–3+ years in operations, account management, or coordination roles at a fast-paced company.

 - Experience working with multi-location, multi-account, or franchise portfolios (e.g., food delivery operators like Rappi, consultancies like PwC/EY/Accenture, or marketplace operators).

 - Strong fluency with operational tools and project management software (ClickUp, Asana, Monday, etc.).

 - Comfort learning new tools quickly — POS systems, inventory management, LMS, and delivery platforms.

 - Strong written communication for cross-team and cross-location coordination.

 - Proactive problem-solver — comfortable flagging issues and proposing solutions, not just executing tickets.

Bonus Points

 - Experience supporting U.S.-based hospitality, food service, or multi-location retail businesses.

 - Familiarity with Toast POS, Super Order, Opus LMS, or restaurant inventory management systems.

 - Experience building or maintaining SOP documentation libraries.

 - Comfort with AI-assisted workflows (ChatGPT, automation tools, agent setup).

 - Experience supporting franchise operations or onboarding new locations.

Details:

 - Work Location: Remote

 - Work Timezone: U.S. (must overlap with U.S. business hours)

 - Compensation: $1,100–$1,500/m

source_jobs.title: Senior Content Editor
source_jobs.description:
Type: Full-Time

Location: Remote

Work Timezone: U.S. / North America

Compensation: $2,800-3,250/month USD



About Marco: Marco connects the top 1% of global professionals with ambitious, fast-growing companies. We believe the future of work is borderless—and that exceptional people come from anywhere.

The Role & Our Client:

Our client is a specialized B2B content agency that helps enterprise companies create sharper, more persuasive content for the stakeholders involved in purchasing their products. What makes them different is that they do not create generic top-of-funnel content, but instead starts with understanding the psychology of each client’s buyer.

They are hiring a Senior B2B Content Writer & Editor to help lead the development of high-quality content for enterprise clients.

This right candidate should have strong experience creating content for B2B enterprise companies, with a clear understanding of the tone, structure, and credibility required to write for professional buyer audiences.

You know how to turn expert knowledge, interviews, research, and positioning into polished content that feels smart, strategic, and decision-maker-ready. They should be comfortable working across multiple formats, shaping content angles, interviewing subject matter experts, editing for clarity and persuasion, and ensuring each piece aligns with the client’s target audience and business goals.


WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING

 - Write and edit high-quality content for B2B enterprise clients across multiple formats

 - Develop content that speaks directly to specific buyer personas and decision-makers

 - Turn research, SME interviews, and source materials into compelling, polished narratives

 - Create content briefs, outlines, interview questions, and narrative angles before drafting

 - Edit drafts for structure, clarity, flow, tone, and strategic alignment

 - Translate complex business or technical topics into clear, credible content for professional audiences

 - Support creation of full-funnel content, including:
   
   - White papers
   
   - Case studies
   
   - Thought leadership articles
   
   - Research-based content
   
   - Blog posts
   
   - Technical or industry explainers
     

 - Conduct interviews with founders, executives, product leaders, and subject matter experts

 - Pull out strong insights, quotes, and narratives from interviews and transcripts

 - Manage deadlines and keep multiple content projects moving at once

 - Collaborate closely with editorial leadership and contribute to a high-quality client delivery process


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - Strong background in content writing and editing for B2B enterprise companies

 - Experience writing for sophisticated buyer audiences in SaaS, software, technology, or other complex B2B industries

 - Ability to match the tone and style expected in enterprise content marketing

 - Excellent writing fundamentals, including structure, clarity, headline framing, and narrative flow

 - Strong editing instincts — able to improve content beyond grammar by sharpening arguments, reorganizing ideas, and tightening positioning

 - Experience turning interviews, research, and source material into polished long-form and mid-funnel assets

 - Ability to understand a target audience quickly and tailor messaging to different personas

 - Confident communicator who can ask smart questions and extract useful insight from SMEs

 - Organized and self-directed, with the ability to manage several moving projects at once

 - Comfortable working during U.S. hours and joining client or internal calls when needed


NICE TO HAVE

 - Familiarity with industries like cybersecurity, cloud, infrastructure, HR tech, healthcare tech, fintech, or adjacent enterprise categories

 - Experience with white papers, case studies, and sales enablement content

 - SEO awareness

 - Familiarity with tools like Google Docs, ClickUp, Airtable, and Slack

Details:

 - Work Location: Remote

 - Work Timezone

source_jobs.title: Content Manager
source_jobs.description:
Type: Full-Time

Location: Remote

Work Timezone: U.S. / North America

Compensation: $2,400-$3,000/month USD



About Marco: Marco connects the top 1% of global professionals with ambitious, fast-growing companies. We believe the future of work is borderless—and that exceptional people come from anywhere.



The Role & Our Client:

Our client is a leading seller of commercial drones and UAV solutions to industry leaders across security, infrastructure, construction, mining, agriculture and more.

They are hiring a Content Marketing Manager to fully lead the content strategy, production, distribution, and performance across the entire customer lifecycle—from first touch to conversion and long-term retention.

This is not a “blog manager” role.

This role is for someone who treats content as a revenue-driving system, not a creative side project. You will be responsible for ensuring content directly contributes to conversions, pipeline, post-sale adoption, and brand authority in the enterprise drone and security solutions market.

We strongly believe deep familiarity with drones/UAS or adjacent technical markets is critical to succeeding in this role. This is complex, high-consideration buying—not generic content marketing.



Responsibilities - What You’ll Do:

 - Own conversion rate optimization for all content assets, analyzing performance and iterating to improve funnel progression from awareness to purchase.

 - Build and manage the company-wide content production calendar, coordinating writers, designers, videographers, and external partners to deliver consistent, high-quality output across channels.

 - Lead content distribution strategy across owned (website, blog), earned (PR, social shares), and paid (social ads, sponsored posts) channels to maximize reach and engagement.

 - Oversee email marketing programs, including nurture sequences, newsletters, product announcements, and post-sale campaigns to drive repeat business and upsell opportunities.

 - Define and evolve brand positioning through messaging guidelines, tone of voice, and visual identity to differentiate DSLRPros in the competitive UAS/security space.

 - Drive keyword optimization across all content, collaborating with SEO team to improve organic search rankings and visibility for high-intent terms.

 - Develop and execute pre-sale content (top/middle-funnel) to generate leads and educate prospects, as well as post-sale content (guides, tutorials, case studies) to drive adoption, retention, and advocacy.

 - Track organic conversions (e.g., demo requests, quote submissions, purchases) from content efforts, attributing revenue impact and optimizing for ROI.

 - Manage content analytics and reporting, delivering monthly dashboards on traffic, engagement, conversions, and revenue contribution to leadership.

 - Identify opportunities for AI-assisted content creation, personalization, and automation to scale production and improve efficiency.



What We’re Looking For:

 - Relevant experience in content marketing, digital media, or journalism, with 3-5 years required; a Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, or a related field preferred but not required.

 - Experience in the drone/UAS or education-focused content creation. - we would really like someone with knowledge of the space. We think it will be hard to be successful in the role without it.

 - Proficiency in content management systems (e.g., Shopify, WordPress), project management tools (e.g., Asana), and analytics platforms (e.g., Google Analytics).

 - Strong leadership, communication, and organizational skills to manage teams and cross-functional projects in a fast-paced environment.



Details:

 - Work Location: Remote

 - Work Timezone: U.S. (PST)

 - Compensation: $2,400-$3,000/month USD

 - Supportive leadership team and growth-oriented culture

source_jobs.title: Business Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
Type: Full-Time

Location: Remote

Work Timezone: U.S. / North America

Compensation: $1,600-$2,200/month USD



About Marco: Marco connects the top 1% of global professionals with ambitious, fast-growing companies. We believe the future of work is borderless—and that exceptional people come from anywhere.

The Role & Our Client:

Our client is a leading seller in commercial drone systems, payloads, and UAS solutions — is hiring a Business Development Representative to drive growth with large enterprise and public-sector organizations.

If you’re a hunter who thrives in outbound, complex sales cycles and loves closing big deals, this is a high-impact role with massive growth opportunity.



Responsibilities - What You’ll Do:

 - Own the full Business Development Sales Cycle: Map out industries, prioritize prospects, build the sales funnel (enrichment, outreach, CRM ownership, pipeline development, negotiate and close clients).

 - Hunt, engage, and close six- and seven-figure deals with enterprise organizations.

 - Prospect high-value accounts in public safety, utilities, energy, construction, mining, and Fortune 1000.

 - Run discovery calls, demos, and solution presentations tailored to mission-critical operations.

 - Deliver accurate forecasting and pipeline reporting in Pipedrive.

 - Lead early sales efforts for new product line launches and emerging drone technologies.

 - Dealer Acquisition and Expansion: Identify and onboard new dealers through market research, LinkedIn outreach, industry events, and lead generation tools. Conduct outreach to authorized DJI dealers and complementary businesses where drones represent a natural upsell opportunity.

 - Relationship Management for Existing Dealers: Maintain strong relationships with our current dealer network through regular communication via calls, emails, and video meetings. Anticipate dealer needs, follow up consistently, and ensure smooth, ongoing collaboration to sustain the high level of trust and service the client is known for.

Must Have:

 - Fluent English: C2 level.

 - 3–7+ years in B2B enterprise sales or business development.

 - Strong outbound motion: cold outreach, sequencing, and multi-channel prospecting.

 - Proven experience closing complex deals with multiple stakeholders.

 - Ability to influence director-level and C-suite decision makers.

 - Excellent communication and presentation skills.

 - Familiarity with modern sales stack tools

Bonus Points

 - Relationships in public safety, utilities, energy, construction, or mining.

 - FAA Part 107 certification or knowledge of commercial drone operations.

 - Experience helping launch or sell new product lines.

Details:

 - Work Location: Remote

 - Work Timezone: U.S. (Must respond to U.S. based clients quickly)

 - Compensation: $1,600-$2,200/month USD

 - Fully remote role with long-term career growth

 - Supportive leadership team and growth-oriented culture

notes: Marco connects top professionals with fast-growing companies, with a focus on remote work.
